articles.title: The SpaceX IPO filing has arrived
articles.summary: SpaceX has finally made the contents of its IPO filing public, weeks ahead of what is expected to be the largest IPO ever.

articles.title: Microsoft’s carbon removal plans aren’t dead after all
articles.summary: Microsoft is responsible for over 90% of the carbon removal market, and reports suggested the company was pausing purchases entirely. This new deal should help assuage the fears of CDR startups.

articles.title: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
articles.summary: OpenAI claims its reasoning model disproved a geometry conjecture unsolved since 1946 — and this time, the mathematicians who exposed its last embarrassing claim are backing it up.

articles.title: IrisGo, a startup backed by Andrew Ng, looks to become the AI desktop buddy you never knew you needed
articles.summary: Initially billed as an "AI butler," Iris watches what happens on a user's desktop and automatically learns how to do tasks for them, its co-founder says.

articles.title: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe
articles.summary: First came the Netherlands, now it's Lithuania. And more European countries appear to be in the queue for Tesla's driver-assistance system.

articles.title: Airbnb gets into hotels, expands AI for host onboarding and customer support
articles.summary: Airbnb will soon let you book luggage storage and car rental services on its app.

articles.title: Global EV market goes K-shaped as the US gets left behind
articles.summary: EV sales have surged worldwide in every region but the U.S., posing risks for legacy and startup automakers.

articles.title: OpenAI barrels toward IPO that may happen in September
articles.summary: A day after Elon Musk lost his lawsuit that threatened OpenAI's structure, leadership, and finances, OpenAI is reportedly back to prepping for its IPO.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos, you were so close to making a good point
articles.summary: "Why is a nurse in Queens who makes $75,000 a year paying more than $1,000 a month in taxes?" Bezos said Wednesday morning on CNBC. "That's $1,000 that could help with rent, or groceries, or anything."

articles.title: Customers say Trump Mobile is leaking their personal information
articles.summary: Trump Mobile is leaking customers’ email and home addresses but has not responded to people alerting the company of the data exposure, according to two YouTubers who said they verified that their leaked data is authentic.

articles.title: Intuit to lay off over 3,000 employees to refocus on AI
articles.summary: In a memo to employees, CEO Sasan Goodarzi said the layoffs are meant to reduce complexity, simplify the company's corporate structure, and deliver better AI products.

articles.title: AI search startups are blowing up
articles.summary: AI search has quietly become one of the most attractive targets in consumer AI.

articles.title: Stability AI releases a new audio model that can create 6-minute songs
articles.summary: Stability Audio 3.0 small model can run on-device and generate two-minute long tracks.

articles.title: NanoClaw creator turns down $20M buyout offer, raises $12M seed instead
articles.summary: NanoClaw was created as a secure alternative to OpenClaw to assist the Cohen brothers with their AI marketing firm that used agents to do much of the work. But instead of running directly on a computer, NanoClaw runs sandboxed in a container.

articles.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in 1 week: Window to nominate and apply for the most promising startups closes May 27
articles.summary: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27. Pitch at TechCrunch Disrupt, get investor access, win $100K, plus more scaling perks. Nominate an early-stage founder or submit your startup today.

articles.title: GitHub says hackers stole data from thousands of internal repositories
articles.summary: The code hosting giant GitHub said it was investigating a breach but said there was no evidence of customer data theft.

articles.title: Figma adds an AI assistant to its collaborative canvas
articles.summary: Figma says users can employ natural language text prompts to direct its new AI agent to generate new designs, edit existing ones, or automate tasks such as generating iterations of existing designs.

articles.title: This startup raised $43M to build a hive mind for ships
articles.summary: The Arlington, Virginia-based startup has raised $42 million to equip ships with sensors that blow the current AIS tech out of the water.

articles.title: ‘Ask YouTube’ brings AI-powered conversational search to video, adds Gemini Omni to Shorts
articles.summary: Google is completely revamping its search experience, and that doesn't stop at YouTube.

articles.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
articles.summary: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.
